title: poa serotina var. nemoralis
intro: Poa serotina var. nemoralis, also known as woodland blue grass, is a perennial grass species in the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of woodland blue grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory and digestive issues.
